---
title: "Paper Process Flowchart"
subtitle: "One-page visual guide for non-academic readers"
author: "Research Team"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This is the shortest visual explanation of how the paper is built. For the full plain-English walkthrough, see `reports/05_process_guide.qmd`.

## The Flow

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A["Locked scope<br/>research_strategy.md<br/>_targets.R"] --> B["LiTS raw files<br/>2010, 2016, 2022-23"]
    B --> C["Harmonize LiTS<br/>R/20_ingest_data.R<br/>lits_harmonized.csv"]
    C --> D["Module A<br/>Descriptive mobility measures<br/>R/30_module_a_mobility.R"]
    D --> E["Tier A packaging<br/>Tables and figures for the paper<br/>R/31_module_a_tier_a_descriptive.R"]
    C --> F["Module B<br/>Pooled associational models<br/>R/40_module_b_determinants.R"]
    B --> G["Module C<br/>LiTS IV child-module extension<br/>R/50_module_c_mechanisms.R"]
    H["HBS raw files"] --> I["HBS support layer<br/>Context + linkage diagnostics<br/>R/31-33 HBS scripts"]
    E --> J["Empirical audit<br/>R/60_empirical_audit.R"]
    F --> J
    G --> J
    I --> J
    E --> K["Main manuscript<br/>reports/00_main.qmd"]
    F --> K
    G --> K
    I --> K
    J --> K
    K --> L["Publication outputs<br/>HTML/DOCX paper and appendix"]
```

## What Each Box Means

| Box | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Locked scope | Fix the question, modules, and rules before writing results. |
| LiTS raw files | The core survey source for the paper's intergenerational evidence. |
| Harmonize LiTS | Convert different waves into one comparable adult analysis file. |
| Module A | Describe mobility levels, transitions, and persistence across waves. |
| Tier A packaging | Turn descriptive outputs into manuscript-ready tables and figures. |
| Module B | Estimate pooled regressions that show conditional associations. |
| Module C | Add a short, non-causal child-module extension for 2022-23. |
| HBS support layer | Provide household context and test whether HBS can support an appendix model. |
| Empirical audit | Check sample flow, formulas, denominators, and whether claims match outputs. |
| Main manuscript | Pull saved output files into the final paper text, tables, and figures. |
| Publication outputs | Render the final products readers actually open. |

## How To Read This As A Beginner

1. Start in the middle, not at the end: the manuscript is assembled from stored outputs rather than typed manually from memory.
2. Treat Module A as the core facts, Module B as the conditional correlates layer, and Module C as a bounded extension.
3. Read HBS as supporting context unless the linkage diagnostics justify something stronger.
4. Use the audit box as the quality-control checkpoint that keeps the paper from drifting away from the code.

## One-Sentence Summary

The paper is built by cleaning and harmonizing LiTS data, estimating three tightly scoped empirical modules, auditing the outputs, and then assembling those saved outputs into the manuscript.
